pages.text: Tobacco quality starts in the field. Every plant builds a hidden quality value while it grows. Curing, fermenting, aging, and finished products all build on that starting leaf quality.

For best results:$(br2)- Let plants fully mature before harvesting$(br)- Give them strong light$(br)- Avoid bad weather and poor environments$(br)- Use $(thing)/tobacconist debug$() while looking at a crop if you are unsure.
Good processing helps good leaf. It does not magically fix bad leaf. Sloppy growing limits everything that comes after.
